name: Quantum Cryptography
use: Secure communication using quantum key distribution.
what: Techniques for generating and distributing encryption keys.

name: Cybersecurity Threats and Defenses
use: Identifying and mitigating cyber threats to critical infrastructure and personal data.
what: Advanced cybersecurity measures are being developed to protect against sophisticated attacks, including AI-driven threat detection and response systems.
how: Organizations are implementing multi-layered security strategies that include encryption, firewalls, and intrusion detection systems.

name: Blockchain Technology for Identity Verification
use: Using blockchain to securely manage digital identities and prevent identity theft.
what: Blockchain-based systems can provide a tamper-proof record of personal information, ensuring privacy and security in online transactions and interactions.
how: Developers are creating decentralized identity solutions that leverage smart contracts and cryptographic techniques.

name: Quantum Cryptography for Secure Communication
what: Quantum cryptography provides unbreakable encryption methods.
how: Ensuring the security of sensitive information and communications.

name: Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)
what: Cryptographic algorithms designed to be secure against quantum computer attacks.
how: Lattice-based and code-based mathematical problems that are computationally hard for Shor's algorithm.

name: Cybersecurity Threats
what: The landscape of cybersecurity is evolving rapidly, with new threats emerging that require advanced protection strategies.
how: Advanced encryption, threat detection

what: Protecting AI systems from cyber threats and ensuring data privacy.

name: Quantum Key Distribution
what: 2-3 specific technical sentences.
how: How it physically works.

name: Cyber Security Advancements
what: Enhances cybersecurity measures to protect against advanced threats.
how: Includes AI-driven threat detection and response systems.

name: Blockchain Innovations
what: Implementing advanced cryptographic techniques to enhance security and transparency.
how: Using zero-knowledge proofs and homomorphic encryption for secure data handling.

name: Fully Homomorphic Encryption
what: Encryption that lets a server compute on data while it stays encrypted, so the server never sees the plaintext.
how: Data is encrypted into lattice-based ciphertexts that support addition and multiplication; periodic 'bootstrapping' keeps noise in check across long computations.
use: Privacy-preserving cloud analytics, confidential machine learning

name: Indistinguishability Obfuscation
what: A theoretical way to scramble a program so it still runs but its internal logic and secrets cannot be recovered.
how: Constructions build on well-studied hardness assumptions to make two functionally identical programs computationally indistinguishable — provably, but far from practical speeds.

name: Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration
what: Replacing today's public-key algorithms with ones believed safe against future quantum computers.
how: Standardised lattice-based schemes are rolled out in hybrid mode alongside classical algorithms so connections stay secure during the transition.
use: Long-lived data protection, TLS and VPN hardening

name: Zero-Knowledge Proof Systems
what: Protocols that let one party prove a statement is true without revealing the underlying data.
how: A prover compiles a computation into a succinct proof (zk-SNARK or zk-STARK) that a verifier can check quickly without re-running the work.
use: Verifiable computation, private authentication, scaling ledgers

name: Quantum Key Distribution Networks
what: City-scale fibre networks that distribute encryption keys using single photons, so eavesdropping disturbs the signal and is detected.
how: Photons in quantum states are sent between nodes; any interception changes the measured statistics, and trusted or twin-field relays extend the reach.
use: High-assurance key exchange for telecom and finance links
